what_it_does: Stores a JSON array of extracted visual/design atoms from pitch decks and landing-page mockups, each annotated with a name, descriptive recipe, evidence pages, an emergent flag, a prescriptive generator_rule, and provenance metadata (_src, _depth).
observations: Each entry is a single design atom with category 'atom'.
Fields are consistent: name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Sources include capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
Compression depths range from 1-page overview to 49-page long-form.
Several atoms recur across sources with slightly different numeric specs (stat card, V badge, gold bullet, QR overlay, star marker).
Some atoms are flagged emergent=true and carry semantic rules (left arrow = result, star = urgency/timing, wordmark on catalogue slides).
The file ends mid-object; the last entry's generator_rule is truncated.
Color codes for 'gold' vary across entries (#C8A84B, #C9A227, #C4A832, #B8960C, #C8960A, #C8A020, #C9A84C, #E0A82C).
Generator rules often use absolute language: 'always', 'never', 'only'.
Evidence is given as page references (p-XX, lpX strip_XX) rather than asset paths.
standouts: Persistent V corner badge appears in almost every source as a mandatory brand anchor.
Gold right-pointing triangle is treated as the universal bullet and forward-motion symbol.
Stat card is repeatedly identified as the primary data atom.
Star symbol is reserved for urgency/timing/milestone signals.
Directional arrow grammar distinguishes right-pointing (feature/forward) from left-pointing (result/inbound).
Property-stage icon set is required to deploy as a complete row, never as a subset.
conventions: JSON array-of-objects structure with uniform keys per atom.
Names are plain-language descriptions rather than token IDs.
Recipe describes visual appearance; generator_rule prescribes usage.
Boolean emergent flag separates recurring atoms from inferred semantic rules.
Provenance is captured in _src and _depth fields.
Unicode symbols (arrows, triangles, stars, checkmarks) are used directly in strings.
concerns: The file is truncated at the final object.
Multiple near-duplicate atoms for the same concept carry conflicting numeric values (sizes, padding, color codes) with no reconciliation field.
Absolute generator rules are sometimes supported by only one or two evidence pages.
No schema version, unique IDs, or normalization of color tokens.
No explicit links from page references to actual image or slide assets.
notes: A cross-deck inventory of recurring visual atoms and their usage rules, extracted from pitch and landing materials, with source and compression-depth provenance.
questions: How are duplicate or conflicting atom specifications reconciled?
Is the emergent flag assigned manually or inferred?
What is the full schema for category beyond 'atom'?
Why is the final object truncated?
How are page references mapped to concrete assets?
What is the intended relationship between recipe and generator_rule?
Are these findings human-curated or model-extracted?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's path and content indicate it is meant to catalog reusable design components; it does so as a JSON list of atoms with recipes, rules, evidence, and provenance, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: Brand consistency through persistent anchors and restricted symbol semantics
Architectural/drafting aesthetic (line-art icons, warm neutrals, gold accents)
Prescriptive visual grammar over flat pattern library
intention_signals: To build a reusable, context-aware design grammar that records not only what each component looks like but when, why, and how it should be generated across different deck depths and audiences.
novelty: The combination of generator_rule, emergent flag, and _src/_depth provenance turns a flat pattern list into a prescriptive, source-attributed design grammar; additionally, directional semantics (right=forward/feature, left=result) and reserved symbol meanings (star=urgency) are unusual for a component ledger.
contribution: Provides a source-attributed, rule-bearing component taxonomy with visual recipes, usage constraints, evidence citations, and provenance metadata that a fused system could keep as design tokens and guardrails.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON array of design atoms extracted from multiple ConceptV pitch decks and landing pages, each with a name, visual recipe, evidence pages, emergent flag, generator rule, and source/depth provenance.
intention_for_embedding: To create a reusable, prescriptive design-component ledger that records how visual atoms are specified and deployed across different compression depths and source decks.
